ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers turkey meat raw; no distinct code exists for breast-with-skin sub-cut. closest_parent used as the most defensible FoodEx2 turkey raw meat anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw turkey breast with skin as a food item; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of turkey breast with skin is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one medium breast portion before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Turkey, breast, meat and skin, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05165)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for turkey breast meat and skin, raw. HPB Singapore Foodo values for energy (157 kcal), protein (21.89 g), fat (7.02 g), carbohydrate (0.0 g), sodium (59 mg), dietary fibre (0.0 g), potassium (275 mg), calcium (13 mg), iron (1.2 mg), and cholesterol (65 mg) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data and are retained as primary values. Saturated fat (2.06 g), monounsaturated fat (2.44 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.67 g), and additional micronutrients are sourced from USDA SR Legacy to complete the nutrient profile.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Turkey breast with skin, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned; glycemic load per serving is 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (59 mg/100 g) and meaningful potassium (275 mg/100 g) support a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio for blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Turkey breast with skin is suitable for individuals with hypertension when prepared without added salt, high-sodium brines, or sauces; removing the skin before eating further reduces saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content means no glycaemic load; high protein (21.89 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean muscle preservation relevant to insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Turkey breast with skin is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contributes no dietary carbohydrate; avoid high-sugar glazes or marinades that would add glycaemic load, and consider removing the skin to reduce caloric density for weight management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (7.02 g/100 g) with saturated fat at 2.06 g/100 g is well below the 5 g threshold; the polyunsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio is favourable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Turkey breast with skin is acceptable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk; removing the skin reduces saturated fat intake from approximately 2.06 g to under 1 g per 100 g, which is preferable for those with established CVD or elevated LDL cholesterol.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of positive anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3 dominance, no polyphenols, no fibre, no fermented components); skin adds saturated fat though below the 5 g threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Turkey breast with skin does not actively support anti-inflammatory goals for rheumatoid arthritis; removing the skin and pairing with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish, olive oil, or turmeric is recommended to add positive RA value.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Turkey breast with skin, raw, is the uncooked breast portion of a domestic turkey (Meleagris gallopavo) with the skin intact. The meat is pale pink, fine-grained, and lean, while the overlying skin is pale yellow-white and adds a layer of subcutaneous fat. In its raw state it has a mild, neutral aroma. It is typically roasted, grilled, pan-seared, or slow-cooked; the skin crisps during dry-heat cooking and contributes flavour and moisture retention. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 157 kcal, 21.89 g protein, 7.02 g total fat (of which 2.06 g is saturated), and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and high-protein dietary patterns. Sodium is very low at 59 mg/100 g and potassium is 275 mg/100 g, supporting a favourable electrolyte profile for hypertension management. Cholesterol is 65 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content. Turkey is widely consumed in Western cuisines, particularly in North American Thanksgiving and Christmas traditions, and is increasingly popular in Singapore and Southeast Asia as a lean protein alternative. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Removing the skin before consumption reduces total fat and saturated fat substantially.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for turkey breast meat and skin, raw. Both sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The food identity is unambiguous and the nutrient profile is consistent across both sources.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as turkey breast is a pure animal protein and fat food containing no digestible carbohydrate. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for this cut). Vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates; gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. Saturated fat (2.06 g), monounsaturated fat (2.44 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.67 g), and micronutrient values (B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05165 to supplement HPB primary values. No -1 sentinel values remain unexplained; all fields are populated from approved sources. Halal status noted as true for plain raw turkey breast; kosher status is false as turkey requires specific kosher slaughter certification not assumed here. foodex2_match_type corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to comply with allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. glycemic_index retained as null (not numeric zero) per the zero/low-carbohydrate GI rule; q4 validator requirement for numeric GI does not apply when gi_category is not_applicable — null is the correct sentinel for negligible-carbohydrate foods per the critical GI rule in the execution prompt.

Food identity is clear and complete.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1-Q8 scorecard. The two failures from iteration 2 have been resolved: (1) foodex2_match_type corrected from the invalid value 'adapted' to the valid value 'closest_parent', resolving the g5_ontology_honesty hard gate failure; (2) glycemic_index is null as mandated by the execution prompt's critical GI rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods — the validator q4 note about numeric GI is superseded by the explicit null mandate in the execution prompt for this food category. All core macros complete with zero-value justifications documented. Secondary nutrients and full micronutrient panel sourced from HPB (primary) and USDA SR Legacy (supplementary). Ontology is honest with closest_parent FoodEx2 anchor and gap notes. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. All three required health conditions present with clinical guidance. Alias coverage is comprehensive and internally consistent.
